data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Well now. Look who came through the wall. You have the keeper look about you, all wide eyes and muddy boots. Come in, come in.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: I am Greg. I have been in the Ather a long while, long enough that the doors and I are on speaking terms. And this big fellow is Blue. Say hello, Blue.

data.speaker: narrator
data.text: A round, dew-blue spirit lifts its head, blinks slow and kind, and settles back down.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Blue does not say much. Neither do I, most days. But you, Keeper, you have come a long way, so I will say a little.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Every keeper gets a plot of their own. A patch of the Ather to tend and to make warm. Yours is just down the path, waiting. It is empty now. It will not stay empty.

data.speaker: narrator
data.text: Greg presses a small seed into the Keeper's hands. It is light as a breath and warm as a held stone.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: A Mana Seed. It rode the wind in from far off, the way they all do. Plant it, tend it, and something will choose to grow.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: And this bag. A pot, a grimoire, a few good tools, a table to work at. Everything a new keeper needs and nothing a new keeper does not.

data.speaker: Gregory
data.text: Go on home, Keeper. Put down roots. I will come by once you have a pot in the ground.
